fieldRegistry.label: Primary Reason For Home Health / Medical Necessity

fieldGroups.label: Medical Necessity And Homebound
fieldGroups.description: Narratives and evidence for home health need and homebound status.

fieldGroups.label: Diagnosis And Coding
fieldGroups.description: Diagnosis evidence, coding-sensitive rows, and escalation flags.

fieldGroups.label: Medications, Allergies, And Special Treatments
fieldGroups.description: Medication safety, allergies, injectables, and O0110 special treatments.

fieldGroups.label: Functional And Therapy Status
fieldGroups.description: GG/M functional scoring, therapy need, discipline frequency, and goals.

fieldGroups.label: Symptom And Body System Review
fieldGroups.description: Body-system review sections that QA checks for consistency and completeness.

fieldGroups.label: Risk And Safety Assessments
fieldGroups.description: Fall, wound, hospitalization, and safety-related risk tools.

fieldGroups.label: Care Plan And Coordination
fieldGroups.description: Plan-of-care orders, patient summary, skilled interventions, and care plan content.

fieldGroups.label: Supplementals And History
fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.label: Low Priority Admin Reference
fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

Worsened.
Physical Exam Constitutional: The patient appears to be stated age, cooperative and in no distress.
The patient is able to answer questions and carry on a conversation without use of accessory muscles of respiration.
The patient is well nourished and hydrated with moist mucous membranes.
The patient moves the upper and lower extremities equally.
Patient appears to have intact judgement and insight into the current health issues.

Hatfield, D.O., 02/12/2026 Careslip ICD10 Encounter Diagnosis: Unilateral primary osteoarthritis, right knee (ICD10CM - M17.11) Procedures Applied on 02/12/2026: Procedure code: office/outpatient visit est (99214) Units: 1.00 Date: 02/12/2026 Unilateral primary osteoarthritis, right knee (ICD10CM - M17.11)
M17.11) Procedures Applied on 02/12/2026
RN - per protocol to assess for pain, incision monitoring and post op complications.
Subjective Complaints: Knee Pain Reevaluation of right Continues to complain of ;
Pain.

R knee OA, pain continues, no significant relief W conservative, management W NSAIDS< injections steroid and HA series, had arthroscopic surgery 2 yrs ago , MM tear and OA noted, has failed conservative management Julianne returns for a Recheck and the condition has;
Musculoskeletal: R knee MJL pain, spurring painful, no laxity, good AROM, no effusion, antalgic gait Extremities: Normal appearance of the upper and lower extremities without obvious deformity.
RN - standing orders for home infusion medication administration protocol x2 on POD 0.
Monitor patient's response to therapy and for side effects/complications to include, medication, pain and incision monitoring.
Allergies: NKDA.
Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Medication: Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Head and Face: Normocephalic/Atraumatic.
Eye: Externally: Normal appearance of the conjunctiva, eyelids with normal ocular symmetry.
Ears, Nose, Mouth, Throat: Normal external appearance of the ears and nose.
Appropriate mood and affect.
Alert and oriented to person, time and place.
Respiratory: Normal respiratory effort without use of accessory muscles of respiration or pursed lip breathing.
Normal external appearance of the chest without barrel chest, pectus, kyphosis or scoliosis.
RN - may straight cath PRN inability to void post op RN - Remove dressing POD 3 and 7, may clean with hydrogen peroxide prn.
Apply dry dressing, may repeat as needed for saturation.
Leave dry dressing in place after POD 7 until MD f/u appt.
Neck: No jugular venous elevation.
Skin: Externally there is no evidence of xanthomas or cellulitis.
There is no evidence of venous stasis changes.

I certify this patient is confined to home and needs intermittent skilled nursing care and or therapy services.

The patient is under my care & I have authorized the services on this plan **SERVICES TO BEGIN ON 03/12/2026 (date) Home Care and Home Infusion Orders PT - pre-operative patient/home evaluation (up to 14 days in advance of the procedure) Provide anaphylactic kit per pharmacist recommendations as needed.
RN - assessment and skilled intervention/care related to infusion therapy, vascular access site and multisystem assessment.
RN - to start/maintain IV catheter per protocol and remove at the conclusion of therapy.

Subjective Findings: A review of systems was performed and was all negative except for the system as documented on the initial visit, those systems associated with the injury, and any changes noted below.
Location The right Knee Changes in Medical History Past Medical History: Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Past Surgical History: Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Family History: Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Social History: Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Review of Systems: Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Sclera are normal without hemorrhage or icterus.
Pupils are equal round and reactive to light and accommodation.
Extraoccular movements are intact.
Normal bilateral auditory acuity.
Normal appearance of the oropharynx without pallor or cyanosis and with moist mucosa.
Normal appearance of the teeth and gums without and dental caries.
Normal appearance of the palate with normal appearance rise of the uvula.
Normal thyroid exam without enlargement, tenderness or nodules.
Normal parotid exam without enlargement, tenderness or nodules.
No palpable masses or nodules.
No palpable No palpable lymphadenopathy in the anterior cervical, posterior cervical, submandibular or supraclavicular areas.
Normal breath sounds without wheezing, rhonchi or rales.
Normal percussive exam without dullness or evidence of consolidation.
Lymphatics: Lymph Nodes Normal.
No evidence of cervical, supraclavicular, axillary, or inguinal adenopathy.
There is no digital clubbing, cyanosis or edema.
This is no dermatitis or rash.
There are no ulcers or wounds present.
The capillary refill is < 5 seconds.
Neurological/Psychiatric: Externally the face is symmetrical.
There is normal sharp and dull sensation in the hands and feet bilaterally.
The cranial nerves are intact II - XII.
The finger to nose exam is normal.
Objective MRI R knee W tricompartmental OA, Impression R knee OA Plan RTKA, risks discussed Signed Electronically By: Stephen E.
